Details: Inactivation of ricin in water by permanganate was found to be very effective at a high doses and short contact time (e.g., permanganate dose of 70,000 mg/L over a 2.5 minute contact time). Treated ricin was injected subcutaneously into a rabbit and showed no toxic effect on the rabbit even though the quantity of ricin injected was equivalent to 5,000 lethal doses of untreated ricin. This suggested that permanganate completely oxidized ricin.
